evidence.text: Sales increased 6.2 percent and 6.3 percent year over year in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2018, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2018 , Compared to Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2017

evidence.text: Pricing contributed less than half of one percentage point to sales growth in each of the three and nine months ended June 30, 2018.
evidence.subsection: Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2018 , Compared to Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2017

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Pricing contributed minimally to sales growth.

evidence.text: Organic sales increased 5.7 percent and 4.8 percent in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2018, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2018 , Compared to Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2017

factors.summary: End markets are growing, particularly in heavy industries and emerging regions.

evidence.text: Sales increased in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2018, mainly due to strength in heavy industries, partially offset by weakness in automotive.
evidence.subsection: Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2018 , Compared to Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strength in heavy industries supports growth.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving.

evidence.text: Segment operating margin increased to 30.0 percent and 29.5 percent in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2018, respectively, primarily due to higher sales.
evidence.subsection: Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2018 , Compared to Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2017

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving margins due to higher sales.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by increased share repurchases.

evidence.text: We repurchased approximately 6.1 million shares of our common stock under our share repurchase program in the first nine months of 2018. On June 11, 2018, we increased our share repurchase target for fiscal year 2018 to $1.5 billion.

Free cash flow was $858.6 million for the nine months ended June 30, 2018, compared to $829.6 million for the nine months ended June 30, 2017. The year-over-year increase was primarily due to an increase in pre-tax income.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving due to lower tax rates.

evidence.text: The effective tax rate for the three months ended June 30, 2018, was 21.1 percent compared to 21.4 percent for the three months ended June 30, 2017. The decrease was primarily due to the impact of the lower U.S. statutory tax rate under the Tax Act.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Lower tax rates improve debt leverage.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing through share buybacks.

evidence.text: We repurchased approximately 6.1 million shares of our common stock under our share repurchase program in the first nine months of 2018. On June 11, 2018, we increased our share repurchase target for fiscal year 2018 to $1.5 billion.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Share buybacks are increasing, indicating improved returns for shareholders.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: Free cash flow was $858.6 million for the nine months ended June 30, 2018, compared to $829.6 million for the nine months ended June 30, 2017. The year-over-year increase was primarily due to an increase in pre-tax income.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow has increased year-over-year, showing improved financial health.
